heading: Coercion of political activity
label: 610 Coercion of political activity
text: It shall be unlawful for any person to intimidate, threaten, command, or coerce, or attempt to intimidate, threaten, command, or coerce, any employee of the Federal Government as defined in , United States Code, to engage in, or not to engage in, any political activity, including, but not limited to, voting or refusing to vote for any candidate or measure in any election, making or refusing to make any political contribution, or working or refusing to work on behalf of any candidate. Any person who violates this section shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than three years, or both.section 7322(1) of title 5
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 610, acts , ; , ; , ; , , ; , , , prohibited campaign contributions or expenditures by national banks, corporations, and labor organizations, prior to repeal by , , .
